_README: Hello AI! Read this file first before making changes. It describes the architecture and responsibilities of this extension.

security_bypass.details: WhatsApp aggressively prevents iframe embedding (anti-clickjacking). We bypass this by sandboxing the iframe in sidebar.html (omitting allow-top-navigation) and modifying request/response headers via rules.json to spoof a direct top-level navigation.

content_scripts.details: content.js was split into multiple files to avoid 800+ lines of spaghetti code. They are injected sequentially. They share state and logic via the `window.WA` object. DO NOT use top-level const/let if they need to be shared, use window.WA instead.

modules.background/background.js: Main extension service worker / event page. Handles action clicks, notification creation, and messaging with the sidebar.
modules.sidebar/sidebar.html: The HTML file rendered in the Firefox sidebar. Contains the sandboxed iframe that loads web.whatsapp.com.
modules.sidebar/sidebar.js: Handles UI interactions inside the sidebar container (e.g. collapse toggle button).
modules.popup/popup.js: Handles the extension popup when clicking the icon (if applicable). Currently mostly delegates to opening the sidebar.
modules.rules.json: DeclarativeNetRequest rules that strip CSP/Frame-Options and spoof Sec-Fetch-* headers to bypass WhatsApp server blocks.
modules.content/state.js: Defines window.WA. Constants, selectors, and shared state (cachedMiniChats, isChatListCollapsed).
modules.content/dom.js: DOM manipulation helpers. Extracts text, rows, names, and times from WhatsApp's React DOM.
modules.content/resizer.js: Adds draggable edges to the WhatsApp chat list and navigation rail to make them resizable inside the sidebar.
modules.content/miniChats.js: Renders floating contact avatars when the left pane is fully collapsed to save space.
modules.content/scanner.js: Scans the DOM for unread message badges and sends updates to background.js.
modules.content/quickReply.js: Simulates keyboard and clipboard events to automatically send messages without user typing.
modules.content/content.js: The main entry point for the content script. Starts Observers and connects all modules.

important_rules: Whenever you modify the responsibility of a file, update this AI_GUIDE.json.
Do not add webRequest blocking logic back to background.js. We use Manifest V3 declarativeNetRequest.
If WhatsApp Web updates its UI, update content/state.js (SELECTORS) first.
All JS files must have a JSDoc block comment at line 1 summarizing what they do.
